_comment: A/B hypothesis presets for /t/:id. Levers: 'vibe' = emotional register (tender | excited | empowered); 'framing' = pain (name the wound, default) | aspiration (future-pace the better/happier state); 'openStyle' = grounded | whisper; 'hookAngle' = '' to derive from the section, or pin a territory; 'maxWords', 'hookCardSecs', 'section' ('random' or a partial title). Server reads this file per request — no restart needed.

t1.label: Aspiration / glow-up (exciting)
t1.hypothesis: Selling the BETTER state on the other side — relief, freedom, the woman she's becoming — with an excited, hopeful tone wins more than dwelling on the wound. The viewer should feel she can be happier than she is now, not just seen in her pain.

t2.label: Excited vibe
t2.hypothesis: Swapping the sad/tender register for an upbeat, hopeful, energised tone (only variable changed vs t1) keeps viewers watching and earns more likes than the heavy emotional version.

t3.label: Boundary-guilt locked
t3.hypothesis: Forcing the proven boundary->apology angle every time beats random angle selection on skip rate and saves.
t3.hookAngle: the second right after she set a boundary — when the apology came tumbling out unprompted

t4.label: Empowered vibe
t4.hypothesis: A steady, certain, fierce 'you are not doing this anymore' register drives more saves (intent) than either the tender or excited tone.

t5.label: Ultra-short punch
t5.hypothesis: Cutting runtime hard (~55 words, 1.5s card) lifts completion rate and therefore reach.
t5.hookAngle: the over-explaining spiral: she answered 'no' and then kept justifying it nobody asked
